title: Currency formatter can display incorrect amounts by converting money values through float
reasoning: The filter converts every value with float(value) before formatting. For Decimal values or large integer balances, binary floating-point conversion can lose precision before the display string is produced, so a stored amount can be rendered as a different rounded whole-dong value. This is especially relevant for a finance template filter because template output may be used to verify balances or transaction totals.
reproduction: Pass Decimal('9007199254740993') or the string '9007199254740993' to format_currency; converting through float cannot represent that value exactly before formatting.
recommendation: Format Decimal or integer values without converting through float. For example, convert with Decimal(str(value)) and quantize/format the Decimal, while preserving the existing fallback for invalid inputs.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this source group, and the excerpt contains no coverage for large numeric strings or Decimal monetary values.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests for format_currency with Decimal and large integer/string monetary values that assert the rendered amount matches the exact original value after whole-unit formatting.
minimumFixScope: Update format_currency to avoid float conversion and add focused tests for exact currency formatting.
